step_description: Check for duplicate or near-duplicate accounts sharing this device_id.
Review posting velocity -- how many comments in the last 24-72 hours?
Check report volume and reason distribution for this account.
Check whether the account is linked to a known campaign cluster.
Escalate if network_density on the linked campaign exceeds 0.6.
Check account creation date -- bot accounts typically cluster within days.
Check device_id and ip_region overlap with other accounts in the cluster.
Review posting cadence for non-human timing patterns.
Cross-reference campaign velocity_score against historical bot campaigns.
Escalate for platform-wide device block if network_density > 0.7.
Read the reported content in full context, not just the flagged excerpt.
Check the target account's report history for a pattern.
Check whether this account has prior harassment cases (repeat behavior).
Determine if multiple reporters are flagging the same target (coordination signal).
Escalate immediately if the content contains threat-level language.
Extract and review any linked URLs from the account's comments.
Check account age vs. posting behavior -- scam accounts are usually new.
Check report_reason distribution for 'scam' or 'impersonation' concentration.
Check for shared device/IP with other scam-flagged accounts.
Escalate for platform-wide link block if the same domain repeats across accounts.
Compare this account's engagement velocity to the campaign's average.
Check device_reuse_signal -- fake engagement clusters usually share devices.
Spot-check a sample of comments for authenticity vs. templated text.
Check campaign similarity_score -- high text similarity supports coordination.
Escalate if velocity_score is sustained (not a single burst).
Pull the account's full prior case history.
Check whether prior violations follow a consistent pattern or escalate in severity.
Review whether the previous resolution actually held (no relapse).
Check current composite_risk_score against the score at last resolution.
Escalate to permanent-action review if this is the 3rd or later offense.
